intro.line: Now then, where were we? Ah yes... the battle of wits.
Has it started? Who's winning?
Nobody can tell yet. That is rather the point of a battle of wits.
Ah, the Dread Pirate Roberts. I was beginning to think you weren't coming.
Now, a battle of wits to the death. *cackles* Poison is on the table. Genius is not — at least on your side.
*sneeze* Is this the part where he figures out the cup?
Not yet. First he must prove himself worthy. There are riddles.
*cough* I like riddles.
You think you can outthink me? ME? The man who orchestrated the kidnapping of the century?
Prepare to be severely humbled. It is going to be magnificent.
Grandpa, why doesn't he just fight him?
Because Vizzini has a knife to Buttercup's throat. Brains are required here, not brawn.
Have you ever heard of Plato? Aristotle? Socrates?
Morons. Absolute morons compared to me. Now, let us begin.
The air was thick with tension. And the smell of iocane powder.
I thought you said iocane powder was odorless?
It is. The tension, however, smelled very strongly of anxiety.
I will give you a sporting chance. A very small one, but sporting nonetheless.
Try not to disappoint me too quickly.
*cough* Is Vizzini really the smartest?
He certainly believed he was. And belief is a powerful, dangerous thing.
Let us establish the rules. I am brilliant. You are not. Those are the rules.
If you forget them, the poison will remind you.

*cough* Grandpa, is Vizzini always this... loud?
He does love the sound of his own voice, yes.
I have never lost a battle of wits. NEVER. It is a point of professional pride.
*sneeze* Sorry. Go on.
Bless you.
You are staring at me blankly. Is the cognitive load too much for you?
I think I know the answer to this one!
Keep it to yourself, then. Let the pirate do his work.
Are you sweating? It looks like you're sweating. How pathetic.
I could do this all day. But I have an empire to run. So hurry up.
The pirate remained completely still. His face a mask of absolute calm.
Was he scared?
Terrified. But he hid it well.
Tick tock, pirate. Tick tock.
I am so bored I might just start drinking the poison myself to liven things up.

I can see the gears turning in your head. They are rusted and slow.

outro_win.line: And so the Dread Pirate Roberts proved his worth. The battle of wits was won.
He beat Vizzini? *cough* But Vizzini's supposed to be the smartest man in the world!
Only in his own estimation, which is the loneliest kind of genius.
Vizzini never did learn the first rule of a battle of wits: never assume you're the smartest one at the table.
*cough* What's the first rule?
I just told you.
Buttercup was rescued. Vizzini was outwitted. And the goblet chose wisely.
Was Vizzini very angry?
In his experience, Vizzini was always angry. It was his natural resting state.
The pirate smiled. It was a good smile. A winning smile.
I knew he'd do it! I never doubted him for a second!
And that, my boy, is why you always check both cups before you drink.
*cough* I'll remember that when I'm drinking my milk.
Vizzini's grand intellect was finally silenced. Permanently.
Did it hurt? The poison?
He barely had time to register his own failure before the end came.
He won! The pirate won! 🏴‍☠️
Indeed. A victory for quiet competence over loud incompetence.
Take that, you arrogant... Sicilian! 😂
Language, my boy. But yes, exactly.

outro_lose.line: Well. That did not go as hoped.
*cough* Does he die?
...Would you like to hear a different part of the story?
Yes please.
Vizzini was, it must be said, very smug about the whole business.
*sneeze* He won?
Briefly. As villains sometimes do. But that is a story for another night.
The iocane powder is odorless, tasteless, and entirely fatal. He should have known.
Maybe next time he'll be more careful.
Next time he will be. Sleep now — we'll try again tomorrow.
A tragic miscalculation. The pirate fell, and Vizzini laughed.

Sometimes the hero stumbles. It makes the getting up all the more impressive.
Vizzini took his prize and left the pirate to his fate.
